commit.message:
ci: add AFL++ iccFromCube job to LibFuzzer workflows

Add afl-fromcube job to both libfuzzer-smoke-test.yml and
cfl-libfuzzer-parallel.yml. Each job:
- Installs afl++ from Ubuntu 24.04 repos (4.09c)
- Clones and builds iccDEV with afl-clang-fast++ (ASAN+UBSAN)
- Runs AFL with seed corpus and dictionary from cfl/
- Uploads crash/hang artifacts
- Reports stats in job summary

Smoke test runs 60s, parallel uses configurable fuzz_seconds.

commit.message:
Fix differentiating GPU Dense layer (#910) (#1044)

* Fix differentiating GPU Dense layer (#910)

Flux's Dense layer on CUDA failed because Mooncake traced into CUBLAS
internals and into NNlib's GPU broadcast fallback, both of which contain
types with no tangent_type/fdata_type methods.

Two primitive rules are added to intercept before the problematic internals:

1. ext/MooncakeCUDAExt.jl — LinearAlgebra.mul!(C, A, B) on CuArrays
   Add @is_primitive + frule!! + rrule!! for 3-arg mul!(C, A, B) on
   CuMaybeComplexArray, covering matrix×matrix and matrix×vector for
   real and complex IEEEFloat. Also uses arrayify in the sum frule!!
   instead of primal/tangent directly.

2. ext/MooncakeNNlibExt.jl — NNlib.bias_act!(identity, x, b) on GPU arrays
   NNlib's ChainRules rrule for bias_act! requires a RuleConfig, which
   @from_rrule cannot supply. On AbstractGPUArray, bias_act! falls back
   to non-in-place x .+ b, so the rule returns a new output array with
   its own gradient buffer and accumulates gradients for x and b.

Tests: mul! cases added to test/ext/cuda/cuda.jl; bias_act! cases added
to test/ext/nnlib/nnlib.jl (covers both CPU and GPU via _rand).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix bias_act! GPU rule to be in-place

With CUDA ≥ 5.9.7, NNlib's bias_act!(identity, x, b) modifies x in-place
(x .+= b) rather than returning a new array. The old frule            {                 echo ___BEGIN___COMMAND_OUTPUT_MARKER___;                 PS1=;PS2=;unset HISTFILE;                 EC=0;                 echo ___BEGIN___COMMAND_DONE_MARKER___0;             } and rrule            {                 echo ___BEGIN___COMMAND_OUTPUT_MARKER___;                 PS1=;PS2=;unset HISTFILE;                 EC=0;                 echo ___BEGIN___COMMAND_DONE_MARKER___0;             }
allocated a fresh output array, which caused the primal check
has_equal_data(x_primal, map(primal, x_x̄_rule)) to fail.

Rewrite both rules following the same in-place pattern used by the mul!
rule in MooncakeCUDAExt:
  and in the pullback restore px from the copy after accumulating gradients.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* style(cuda): replace TODO comment with cross-reference

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Apply suggestions from code review

Signed-off-by: Hong Ge <3279477+yebai@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix bias_act! rule to cover CPU arrays too

The @is_primitive / frule            {                 echo ___BEGIN___COMMAND_OUTPUT_MARKER___;                 PS1=;PS2=;unset HISTFILE;                 EC=0;                 echo ___BEGIN___COMMAND_DONE_MARKER___0;             } / rrule            {                 echo ___BEGIN___COMMAND_OUTPUT_MARKER___;                 PS1=;PS2=;unset HISTFILE;                 EC=0;                 echo ___BEGIN___COMMAND_DONE_MARKER___0;             } were registered only for
AbstractGPUArray, but the test asserts is_primitive=true for both CPU
and GPU arrays.  Switch from the local GPUIEEEArray alias to the
existing SupportedArray{<:IEEEFloat} (= Union{Array, AbstractGPUArray}),
which covers both.  Also fix the now-stale test comment.

commit.message:
fix(py): stream_iter() now propagates system_prompt and tools (#80)

stream_iter() was creating AgentStream with only temperature/max_tokens,
using the low-level stream_generate() instead of agent_stream(). This
caused system_prompt, tools, and all other agent options to be silently
dropped during streaming.

Changes:
- AgentStream.__init__: accepts agent_name and options dict
- AgentStream.__aiter__: uses agent_stream (with full options) when
  options are provided; falls back to stream_generate for legacy compat
- Agent.stream_iter: now passes _build_options() to AgentStream
- StreamEvent.from_json: also checks 'delta' field (not just 'data'),
  and captures 'text' from 'done' events

fix: replace unsafe ExecException cast with type guard in execute()

The catch block was using `err as ExecException` — an unsafe cast that
bypasses TypeScript's unknown-type narrowing. Replace with a structural
type guard:

  const execErr =
    typeof err === 'object' && err !== null && 'code' in err
      ? (err as ExecException)
      : null;

Then access all ExecException fields (code, stdout, stderr, signal, killed)
via optional chaining with the same `typeof` guards that were already in
place, falling back to safe defaults when execErr is null.

In practice exec() always rejects with ExecException on command failure, but
the null branch makes the code safe for any unexpected error shape.

Fixes 🔴 Critical finding from TypeScript review (step_19 workflow log).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add KGP app-level support: occlusion, scaling, and demo (#224)

* Add KGP support to surface cell infrastructure (Phase 1)

- Add TrackedObject<KgpCellData>? Kgp field to SurfaceCell with HasKgp property
- Add KGP tracking to TrackedObjectStore (GetOrCreateKgp with content-hash dedup)
- Add KGP count tracking to Surface (HasKgp, mirroring HasSixels pattern)
- Add KGP comparison to SurfaceComparer (KgpEqual by content hash)
- Parameterize ZIndex in KgpCellData.BuildPlacementPayload() (was hardcoded -1)
- Add ZIndex property to KgpCellData, preserved through WithClip()
- Add 19 tests in KgpSurfaceCellTests.cs
- Create KgpDemo sample scaffold with Scene 1 (KGP cell data basics)
- Register KgpDemo in apphost.cs

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add capability propagation through surface render context (Phase 2)

- SurfaceRenderContext now inherits capabilities from parent via SetCapabilities()
- Both caching and non-caching RenderChild paths propagate _capabilities to children
- SurfaceLayerContext gains Capabilities property, passed from SurfaceNode
- SurfaceNode.Render passes context.Capabilities to SurfaceLayerContext constructor
- Add 5 tests in KgpCapabilityPropagationTests.cs
- Add Scene 2 (capability detection) to KgpDemo

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Phase 3: KGP surface compositing

- Add ClipKgpCell in Surface.Composite() for boundary clipping using
  KgpCellData.WithClip() (metadata-only, no image re-encoding)
- Add KGP handling to CompositeSurface cell resolution (both fast and
  computed paths) — KGP cells treated as opaque, preserved through
  transparent layers
- Create KgpVisibility tracker for occlusion tracking and multi-placement
  generation using PixelRect.Subtract for region math
- Add KgpZOrder enum (BelowText/AboveText) for z-ordering API
- Add CreateKgp() factory methods to SurfaceLayerContext (from KgpCellData
  or raw pixel data with RGBA32 encoding)
- Add 26 tests in KgpSurfaceCompositingTests covering: surface clipping,
  composite layer resolution, KgpVisibility occlusion/placement generation,
  SurfaceLayerContext.CreateKgp, and Surface.HasKgp tracking
- Add Scenes 3–4 to KgpDemo (z-ordering model, clipping with WithClip)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Phase 4: SurfaceComparer KGP token emission

- Add KGP region scanning in SurfaceComparer.ToTokens() parallel to sixel
  handling: scan surface for KGP cells intersecting dirty cells
- Emit below-text KGP placements (z < 0) BEFORE text tokens
- Emit above-text KGP placements (z > 0) AFTER text tokens
- Add IsCoveredByKgpRegion() to skip cells under KGP regions
- Handle KGP anchor cells in the per-cell loop (track region, emit or defer)
- Add 12 tests in KgpComparerTokenTests covering: emission, z-ordering,
  caching, clipping, position tokens, SVG round-trip
- Add Scenes 5-6 to KgpDemo (token emission model, image reuse)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Phase 5: KGP Widget and Node API

Add KgpImageWidget (immutable record) and KgpImageNode (mutable class)
following the standard widget/node pattern:

- KgpImageWidget: ImageData, PixelWidth/Height, Fallback, Width/Height, ZOrder
- KgpImageNode: capability-dispatched rendering (KGP transmit+place or fallback),
  pixel-to-cell measurement via CellMetrics, dirty tracking
- KgpImageExtensions: fluent API (KgpImage, AboveText, BelowText, WithWidth, WithHeight)
- 16 tests covering measurement, rendering, z-ordering, capability fallback,
  reconciliation, and SVG round-trip
- KgpDemo Scenes 7-8: widget API usage and above/below text comparison

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Phase 6: KGP in Surface Layers

Add KGP awareness to the surface layer system:

- ISurfaceSource: add HasKgp property (parallel to HasSixels)
- CompositeSurface: add HasKgp, FindKgpAtPosition on LayerResolutionContext
- ComputeContext: add HasKgpBelow(), GetKgpBelow(), GetKgpBelowAt() for
  computed layers to query KGP images from lower layers
- KgpCellAccess: read-only accessor struct with image metadata (ImageId,
  SourcePixelWidth/Height, WidthInCells/HeightInCells, ZIndex,
  CellOffsetX/Y, IsAnchor)
- 20 tests covering ISurfaceSource.HasKgp, draw layer KGP compositing,
  ComputeContext KGP queries, cross-layer z-ordering, and SVG round-trip
- KgpDemo Scenes 9-10: surface layer integration and computed layer effects

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Phase 7: KGP integration tests and demo polish

Add end-to-end integration tests exercising the full pipeline:
- KgpImageWidget → Hex1bApp → render → terminal lifecycle
- Fallback rendering when KGP is not supported
- KGP in VStack with other widgets
- Below/above text z-ordering through Hex1bApp
- Direct render path SVG verification (KGP → terminal → <image>)
- Multiple KGP images in a widget tree

7 integration tests, all 10 KgpDemo scenes complete.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Rewrite KgpDemo as interactive TUI application

Replace console-output-only demo with a proper interactive Hex1b TUI app:
- 5 navigable scenes using Picker widget for scene selection
- Scene 1: KGP image display with gradient and checkerboard
- Scene 2: Z-ordering (below-text vs above-text)
- Scene 3: Surface layer compositing with draw layers
- Scene 4: Multiple KGP images in an HStack layout
- Scene 5: Computed layer effects with gradient background

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix KGP rendering through Hex1bApp surface path

KgpImageNode.RenderKgp() was writing raw APC escape sequences via
context.Write(), but SurfaceRenderContext.ParseEscapeSequence() silently
discards all APC sequences. KGP data never reached surface cells and was
never emitted to the terminal.

Fix: Add WriteKgp() method to the render context hierarchy:
- Hex1bRenderContext (base): writes raw APC transmit+place sequences
  directly to terminal (non-surface path)
- SurfaceRenderContext (override): creates KgpCellData, stores in
  TrackedObjectStore, populates anchor cell with Kgp metadata and fills
  remaining region cells with spaces. SurfaceComparer.ToTokens() then
  reads cell.Kgp data and emits proper tokens with z-ordering.

KgpImageNode.RenderKgp() now calls context.WriteKgp() which works
correctly through both the direct and surface render paths.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix KGP capability propagation through Hex1bApp

Two issues prevented KGP images from rendering in Hex1bApp:

1. ConsolePresentationAdapter.Capabilities did not include SupportsKgp.
   KgpImageNode checks context.Capabilities.SupportsKgp and falls back
   to text when false. Added SupportsKgp = true (safe: KGP uses q=2
   quiet mode so non-supporting terminals ignore the sequences).

2. Hex1bApp.RenderFrameWithSurface() never called SetCapabilities() on
   the SurfaceRenderContext, so it defaulted to TerminalCapabilities.Modern
   which also lacks SupportsKgp. Added surfaceContext.SetCapabilities(caps).

Also added .WithWorkloadLogging() to KgpDemo for diagnostic visibility.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix KGP rendering through Hex1bApp pipeline

Three bugs fixed:

1. AnsiTokenSerializer/AnsiTokenUtf8Serializer had no KgpToken case,
   causing ArgumentException when workload logging filter tried to
   serialize KGP tokens for hex dump. This crashed the terminal's
   output pump task, silently dropping all subsequent output.

2. SurfaceComparer emitted combined transmit+placement APC sequences
   as a single UnrecognizedSequenceToken. NormalizePreTokenizedTokens
   could only extract the first APC (transmit), losing the placement.
   Now emits separate tokens for transmit and placement.

3. Added KgpToken serialization to both string (AnsiTokenSerializer)
   and UTF-8 (AnsiTokenUtf8Serializer) serializers, producing proper
   APC G sequences (ESC _ G controlData ; payload ESC \).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix KGP capability detection and test assertions

- Replace hardcoded SupportsKgp=true with environment-based detection:
  checks TERM (kitty), TERM_PROGRAM (Kitty/WezTerm/Ghostty), and
  HEX1B_KGP env var for explicit opt-in/out
- Fix 2 test assertions for separate transmit/placement token emission
- Update KgpDemo to generate a 64x32 gradient with fallback text

Non-KGP terminals now correctly render fallback text instead of
dumping raw base64 APC content as gibberish.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Make KGP strictly opt-in and remove C=1 virtual placement

- KGP now only enabled via HEX1B_KGP=1 env var (no auto-detection)
  to prevent base64 gibberish in non-supporting terminals
- Remove C=1 from placement command — virtual placement requires
  Unicode placeholder characters which we don't write; without C=1
  the image renders directly at cursor position

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix KGP chunked transfer and remove C=1 from base render context

KGP protocol requires payloads >4096 bytes to be split into multiple
APC sequences using m=1/m=0 continuation flags. Our 64x32 RGBA image
produces ~11KB of base64 which was sent in a single APC, causing
KGP-supporting terminals to truncate the payload and display gibberish.

Changes:
- Add KgpCellData.BuildTransmitChunks() that splits base64 into <=4096
  byte chunks with proper m=1/m=0 framing
- Update SurfaceComparer.EmitKgpTokens() to emit chunked transmit tokens
- Update Hex1bRenderContext.WriteKgp() with chunked transfer and remove
  C=1 virtual placement flag (matches prior KgpCellData fix)
- Remove temporary diagnostic Console.Error.WriteLine from KgpImageNode

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Expand KgpDemo with windowing and occlusion scenarios

Comprehensive demo that exercises KGP graphics in various composition
scenarios:
- KGP image as WindowPanel background (with ASCII art fallback)
- Gradient and checkerboard KGP images inside draggable windows
- Plain text windows for testing occlusion of background KGP
- Multiple procedurally generated test images (gradient, checker, circle)
- Mouse support for window dragging

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Emit KGP delete commands when images move or disappear

KGP placements persist in the terminal until explicitly deleted with
a=d commands. When a window containing a KGP image is dragged, the
image at the old position was never cleaned up, leaving ghost graphics
on screen.

Changes:
- Add SurfaceComparer.ToTokens() overload accepting previousSurface
- Compare previous vs current KGP anchor positions by image ID
- Emit a=d,d=i (delete by image ID) for images that moved or disappeared
- Pass _previousSurface from Hex1bApp.RenderFrameWithSurface()
- Add exception logging to KgpDemo window builders and rescue handler
  (writes to /tmp/kgp-demo-errors.log for debugging)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add KgpPlacementTracker for cross-frame KGP lifecycle management

The new KgpPlacementTracker manages KGP image transmission and placement
across render frames with three key optimizations:

- Transmit-once: images are sent to the terminal only on first appearance
  and cached via content hash tracking
- Placement diffing: compares desired vs previous placements to emit only
  necessary delete/place commands (unchanged placements emit nothing)
- Z-order separation: returns (BeforeText, AfterText) token lists for
  proper interleaving with text rendering

Integration points:
- Hex1bApp.RenderFrameWithSurface() uses the tracker to generate KGP
  commands and merges them with text tokens from SurfaceComparer
- SurfaceComparer.ToTokens() gains skipKgpEmission parameter to avoid
  double emission while preserving KGP region detection for text skipping
- Tracker resets on terminal resize (surface recreation)

Tests: 10 unit tests + 2 integration tests with SVG round-trip verification

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add KGP occlusion solver for image shredding around overlapping windows

Implements a registry + solver architecture that captures KGP image positions
during rendering (before compositing flattens them), then computes visible
rectangular fragments via rectangle subtraction. Lower-z images are
automatically split into non-overlapping strips around higher-z windows,
using KGP source-rect cropping parameters.

Components:
- KgpFragment: record struct for visible image portions with clip coordinates
- KgpImageRegistry: side-channel collecting images and occluder rects per layer
- KgpOcclusionSolver: rectangle subtraction producing 0-4 strips per occluder
- SurfaceRenderContext: propagates registry and layer info to child contexts
- WindowPanelNode: pushes layers and registers window bounds as occluders
- KgpPlacementTracker: refactored for fragment-based dirty checking
- Hex1bApp: wires registry → solver → tracker in render pipeline

All 6035 tests pass (1 pre-existing failure unrelated to KGP).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add KGP occlusion integration tests

8 tests covering the full occlusion pipeline:
- WindowPanel with KGP background, no windows (full image placed)
- Text window overlapping KGP background (image shredded into fragments)
- KGP image inside a window (placed correctly)
- Multiple KGP images in VStack (both rendered with distinct IDs)
- Full pipeline unit test: registry → solver → tracker across 3 frames
- Fully occluded image produces delete commands
- Same-layer images don't occlude each other
- Clip coordinates mapped correctly (4 strips with pixel-level verification)

All 6043 tests pass (1 pre-existing failure unrelated to KGP).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix character artifacts when dragging windows over KGP images

When skipKgpEmission is true (KGP handled externally by tracker), the
SurfaceComparer was still skipping text cell emission for cells covered
by KGP regions. This meant stale window text wasn't cleared when a
window moved, leaving artifacts visible over below-text KGP images.

The fix: only skip KGP-covered cells when the comparer handles KGP
placement itself. When external (skipKgpEmission=true), always emit
clears so the terminal buffer is updated and KGP images show through.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix scaled KGP image shifting during occlusion

Integer division truncation in clip coordinate mapping caused adjacent
fragments to not tile perfectly. E.g., 128px in 30 cells: a fragment
at offset 10 got clipW=floor(5*128/30)=21, but the next fragment at
offset 15 started at clipX=floor(15*128/30)=64, leaving a 1px gap
(21+42=63 vs 64).

Fix: derive clipW from endpoint differences (clipEndX - clipX) instead
of computing width independently. This ensures adjacent fragments abut
exactly with no gaps or overlaps.

Also adds a tiling verification test with non-integer pixel/cell ratios.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Make KGP images scale with window resize

KgpImageNode.RenderKgp() now uses Bounds dimensions instead of fixed
RequestedWidth/Height, so KGP images scale when their container resizes.
MeasureCore also respects SizeHint.Fill to expand into available space.

Updated KgpDemo: all windows are now resizable with .Resizable(), and
KGP images use .Width(SizeHint.Fill).Height(SizeHint.Fill) to scale
dynamically with the window.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix KGP rendering regressions from resize support

Two issues introduced by the previous commit:

1. MeasureCore used constraints.MaxWidth for Fill without guarding against
   int.MaxValue (from VStack's unbounded first-pass measurement), causing
   the image to claim enormous dimensions.

2. RenderKgp unconditionally used Bounds.Width even for fixed-size images
   (where width: 40 was explicitly set), causing them to expand to the
   entire panel area and overlap buttons.

Fix: RequestedWidth/Height (explicit fixed values) now take priority.
Bounds are only used when RequestedWidth is null (dynamic/Fill sizing).
MaxWidth/MaxHeight are only used for Fill when < int.MaxValue.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Revamp KgpDemo with MenuBar, InfoBar, and photo images

Replace button bar with proper MenuBar (File > Generated submenu +
photo images from the sixel-era Website images). Add InfoBar at bottom
with keyboard shortcut hints.

New features:
- File menu with Generated submenu (Gradient, Checkerboard, Circle)
- Photo images (Bonny, Bogie, Mitch, Firestarter) loaded via SkiaSharp
- Text Window option for occlusion testing
- All windows resizable with KGP images using SizeHint.Fill
- Quit menu item

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Register menu popup as KGP occluder

MenuPopupNode.Render() now pushes a KGP occlusion layer and registers
its bounds as an occluder, matching the WindowPanelNode pattern. This
ensures background KGP images are properly shredded around menu dropdowns
instead of showing through the popup.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add KGP image scaling modes (KgpImageStretch)

Introduce KgpImageStretch enum with four modes following WPF conventions:
- Fill: stretch to fill allocated space (default, backward compatible)
- None: natural pixel-to-cell dimensions without scaling
- Uniform: scale to fit maintaining aspect ratio (pair with AlignWidget)
- UniformToFill: scale to fill maintaining aspect ratio with source-rect crop

Key changes:
- KgpImageWidget gains Stretch property with Fill default
- KgpImageNode.MeasureCore handles each mode: None uses natural size,
  Uniform computes fitted dimensions, Fill/UniformToFill expand to fill
- RenderKgp computes source-rect clip for UniformToFill via
  ComputeUniformToFillClip() using cell pixel aspect ratio (10x20)
- WriteKgp gains optional clipX/Y/W/H parameters threaded through
  both Hex1bRenderContext and SurfaceRenderContext to KgpCellData
- Extension methods: .Uniform(), .UniformToFill(), .Stretched(),
  .NaturalSize(), .WithStretch()
- 22 new tests covering all modes, aspect ratio math, and dirty tracking

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add stretch mode selector buttons to KgpDemo image windows

Each image window now shows Fill/Fit/Cover/None buttons that toggle
between KgpImageStretch modes. The active mode is shown with [brackets].
Per-window state is captured via closures.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Rename KgpImageStretch to match standard graphics conventions

Rename enum values for clarity:
- Uniform → Fit (maintain aspect ratio, maximize one dimension)
- UniformToFill → Fill (maintain aspect ratio, crop excess)
- Fill → Stretch (distort to fill all dimensions)
- None unchanged (native size, clip)

Default is now Stretch (backward compatible). Demo defaults to Fit
and shows Fit/Fill/Stretch/None buttons with matching labels.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix stretch modes: RenderKgp now respects scaling mode

MeasureCore was computing mode-specific layout sizes, but RenderKgp
always used Bounds dimensions — making None, Fit, and Stretch look
identical when SizeHint.Fill was set.

Fix: MeasureCore now always claims fill space (layout allocation is
mode-independent). RenderKgp computes mode-specific cell dimensions:
- None: natural pixel-to-cell size, ignoring Bounds
- Fit: fitted within Bounds maintaining aspect ratio
- Stretch: full Bounds (may distort)
- Fill: full Bounds with source-rect crop

Also rename ComputeUniformToFillClip → ComputeFillClip.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix None stretch mode: clip to Bounds at native resolution

When the image's natural cell size exceeds the node's Bounds, cap the
display cells to Bounds and use KGP source-rect clipping to show only
the top-left portion at native pixel density. Previously the image
extended beyond Bounds unconstrained.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix KGP anchor cell character artifact

When skipKgpEmission=true (external tracker), the anchor cell's space
character was never emitted because the continue was unconditional.
This left stale text visible under the top-left corner of KGP images.

Now only skips the anchor cell when the comparer handles KGP directly.
When the tracker handles placement externally, the space falls through
to normal text emission, clearing any stale content.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add alignment and sizing controls to KgpDemo

Each image window now has:
- Stretch mode picker (replaces button row)
- Align checkbox with H/V alignment pickers (Left/Center/Right, Top/Center/Bottom)
- Size checkbox with width/height text inputs for explicit Fixed sizing

When Align is enabled, the KgpImage is wrapped in an AlignWidget with
Fill size hints so the image is positioned within the available space.
When Size is enabled, explicit SizeHint.Fixed dimensions are applied.
Both can be combined to test sized images at specific alignments.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Replace Pickers with ToggleSwitches in KgpDemo

Picker widgets require a popup host which isn't available inside
WindowPanel contexts, causing 'No popup host found' crashes.
ToggleSwitches render inline and work correctly in all contexts.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix CheckboxWidget toggled event reporting wrong state

CheckboxNode.Toggle() changes State before calling ToggledCallback.
The 3-parameter CheckboxToggledEventArgs constructor then reads the
already-toggled node.State and computes NewState as its inverse —
giving handlers the opposite of the actual new state.

Fix: pass previousState and newState explicitly from Toggle() to the
callback so event args always report the correct states.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Move controls into DragBarPanel and fix explicit sizing

Restructure KgpDemo image windows: the image fills the top area,
and all controls (stretch, align, size, close) live in a resizable
DragBarPanel at the bottom with a drag handle on the top edge.

Fix explicit sizing: use KGP-specific .WithWidth()/.WithHeight()
(sets RequestedWidth/RequestedHeight) instead of SizeHint.Fixed
which KgpImageNode's rendering doesn't consume.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Move DragBarPanel above image in KgpDemo

Place the controls DragBarPanel as the first VStack child so its
drag handle appears at the bottom edge, with the image filling
the remaining space below.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add maintain aspect ratio checkbox to size controls

When Size is enabled, an AR (aspect ratio) checkbox appears (on by
default). When checked, height is computed from the width input using
the source image pixel ratio adjusted for terminal cell aspect
(~10px wide, ~20px tall). The height textbox is hidden when AR is on.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Clamp KGP image to Bounds so it never overflows container

When RequestedWidth/RequestedHeight exceed the node's Bounds (e.g.
window resized smaller than the explicit image size), the image now
clips to the visible portion using source-rect clipping. This applies
uniformly to all stretch modes (None, Fit, Fill, Stretch).

Refactored RenderKgp to eliminate early returns — all modes flow
through a single bounds-clamping step before the final WriteKgp call.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix KGP image alignment by not inflating size with fallback

KgpImageNode.MeasureCore was returning Max(fallbackSize, kgpSize).
When the fallback text was wider than the requested KGP dimensions
(e.g. 40-char fallback vs 20-cell image), the inflated measured
width made AlignNode compute the wrong offset for Right/Center
alignment. The fallback now simply renders within the KGP bounds.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Tighten KGP API accessibility per API review

Make pipeline implementation types internal:
- KgpImageRegistry, KgpOcclusionSolver, KgpFragment, KgpPlacementTracker
- KgpImageEntry, OccluderEntry record structs

Make KgpCellData members internal (constructor, BuildPlacementPayload,
BuildTransmitChunks, WithClip, FromPayload, ContentHash, HashEquals).
The type itself stays public since KgpCellAccess.Data exposes it to
Surface compute layer users for metadata inspection.

Tests still access internals via InternalsVisibleTo.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add missing XML documentation to public KGP APIs

Add XML doc comments to:
- KgpPlacement constructor (all 13 parameters)
- KgpImageData constructor (all 6 parameters)
- KgpImageNode.MeasureCore, ArrangeCore, GetFocusableNodes (inheritdoc)
- KgpImageNode.Render (custom summary)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Guard KGP rendering against zero-dimension bounds

Add early returns and guards in KgpImageNode.RenderKgp to prevent
DivideByZeroException when bounds collapse to zero during resize.
This is the likely root cause of windows disappearing when the
terminal is resized: Fill-layout KGP images can get zero-height
bounds momentarily, causing division by zero in the clipping and
aspect ratio calculations.

Guards added:
- Early return when Bounds.Width/Height <= 0
- Early return when PixelWidth/PixelHeight <= 0
- Guard cellWidth/cellHeight > 0 before clipping division
- Guard all inputs > 0 in ComputeFillClip
- Guard cellWidth/cellHeight > 0 before WriteKgp call

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Clip KGP images to surface bounds in WriteKgp

When a KGP image extends beyond its containing surface (e.g., a window
that is taller than the terminal), the placement would overflow and
overlay unrelated UI elements like the MenuBar and InfoBar.

Fix: WriteKgp now clips cellWidth/cellHeight to the surface dimensions
and recomputes pixel-level clip coordinates proportionally, so the
registry and occlusion solver only see the visible portion.

Added integration test verifying KGP placements don't overflow terminal
bounds when images are in constrained VStack layouts.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add hierarchical KGP clip rect to SurfaceRenderContext

The previous surface-bounds clipping only restricted KGP images to the
terminal (root surface) dimensions. In the non-caching render path,
children render directly into the parent surface without compositing,
so there was no mechanism to clip images to intermediate container
bounds (e.g., a WindowPanel that is shorter than the terminal).

Fix: Track a _kgpClipRect (in absolute coordinates) that accumulates
the intersection of every ancestor node's Bounds as RenderChild
descends the tree. WriteKgp applies this clip rect after the surface-
bounds check, handling all four edges including left/top clipping with
proper pixel-coordinate adjustment.

The clip rect is propagated through all three RenderChild paths:
- Non-caching + layout provider (child context init)
- Non-caching direct (save/restore around render)
- Caching cache-miss (child context init)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add KgpImage widget documentation

- Create guide/widgets/kgpimage.md with sections for basic usage,
  fallback content, stretch modes, sizing, z-ordering, file loading,
  and surface integration
- Add code snippets for fallback and stretch mode examples
- Generate static SVG previews for StaticTerminalPreview components
- Create KgpImageBasicExample WebSocket example for live demos
- Register example in Program.cs
- Add KgpImage to Display Widgets section in widgets index

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Move KGP occlusion into surface compositing layer

Replace node-level IsKgpOccluder with automatic compositing-based
occlusion. Every Surface.Composite() call in RenderChild() now pushes
a KGP layer and registers the composited child's bounds as an occluder.

The layer push happens BEFORE the child renders so the child's own
KGP images land on the same layer as the occluder (preventing
self-occlusion) while images at lower layers get properly shredded.

Nodes no longer have any KGP knowledge — IsKgpOccluder, PushKgpLayer,
and KgpRegistry are either removed or made private to SurfaceRenderContext.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Revert CheckboxWidget callback changes, fix event args constructor

Revert the expanded callback signature from commit 76ee3d9 — consumers
should use the controlled component pattern (toggle external state in
the callback) rather than reading ev.NewState.

Fix the 3-parameter CheckboxToggledEventArgs constructor: node.State
has already been toggled when the callback fires, so NewState should
read node.State directly and PreviousState should be derived.

Update KgpDemo to use the controlled component pattern.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix KGP images disappearing when menu popups open

The BackdropNode (transparent popup overlay) was being registered as a
full-screen KGP occluder, hiding all images beneath it. This happened
because RegisterCompositeAsOccluder used the node's layout bounds rather
than the actual rendered content area.

Changes:
- Surface: track written content bounding box (WrittenContentBounds)
  across all cell-write paths (indexer, WriteText, WriteChar, Fill,
  FillBackground, Composite)
- SurfaceRenderContext: register occluders AFTER rendering using the
  child surface's actual content bounds, not the node's full bounds.
  PushKgpLayer still happens before rendering for correct layer
  assignment.
- KgpImageRegistry: add RegisterOccluder overload with explicit layer
  parameter for deferred registration
- Add MenuPopup_DoesNotOccludeBackgroundKgpImage integration test

For opaque nodes (windows with FillBackground), WrittenContentBounds
equals the full surface bounds — behavior unchanged. For transparent
nodes (BackdropNode), only the actual content area (e.g. the menu
popup) is registered as an occluder.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix KGP occlusion layer mismatch for nested compositing

The occluder layer was saved from _kgpLayer (context-local) instead of
_kgpRegistry.CurrentLayer (global). When children rendered via path 2
push additional layers on the shared registry, _kgpLayer falls behind
registry._currentLayer. This caused menu popup occluders to be at the
same layer as the background KGP image (no occlusion) instead of a
higher layer (proper slicing).

Fix: save registry.CurrentLayer right after PushKgpLayer for the
deferred occluder registration. This ensures the occluder layer
accurately reflects the global ordering.

Updated test to verify the image is actually sliced (more placements
after menu open than before).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix KGP window disappearing after drag + terminal resize

On resize, \x1b[2J clears text cells but KGP placements are terminal-level
state that persists until explicitly deleted. After dragging a KGP-containing
window, old placements at stale positions remained visible and interfered
with the re-rendered layout.

Fix: send KGP delete-all (\x1b_Ga=d,d=a,q=2\x1b\) before screen clear on
resize. Added HasEverTransmitted flag to KgpPlacementTracker that survives
Reset() to correctly detect when cleanup is needed.

Added two new integration tests:
- KGP window drag+resize survival (with KGP image)
- Non-KGP window drag+resize control test

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add app-level KGP handling and builder API

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Harden editor rendering test waits

ci: remove agent-types from Codecov upload

agent-types is a pure type-definitions package with no executable code.
Jest produces an empty lcov.info for it, which causes Codecov to reject
the entire upload batch. Only upload coverage for packages with real code.
